title: Portugal: 2026 World Cup Squad, Fixtures and Schedule
original_text:
Cristiano Ronaldo will captain Portugal at a record sixth World Cup, the milestone he shares with longtime rival Lionel Messi.

The 41-year-old arrives at what is widely expected to be his international swansong. Portugal travel to North America as reigning Nations League champions, chasing a first World Cup title. Roberto Martínez announced his 26-man squad on 19 May at Cidade do Futebol. One of the most poignant tributes in modern football accompanied the selection.

Portugal coach named the late Diogo Jota as “the plus one forever” in honour of the Liverpool forward. Jota, just 28, was killed in a car crash in Zamora, Spain, last July, alongside his brother André Silva. He had earned 49 caps and scored 14 international goals.



Martínez also took the rare step of selecting fourth-choice goalkeeper Ricardo Velho as a 27th squad member. The Gençlerbirliği stopper will travel as cover but can only join the official roster in case of injury to one of the three registered keepers.

Ronaldo, Portugal’s all-time top scorer with 143 international goals, missed the qualifying finale against Armenia through suspension. Portugal still ran out 9-1 winners to seal their World Cup ticket. Bruno Fernandes pulls the strings in midfield.

A PSG core of Vitinha, João Neves and Nuno Mendes arrives on a Champions League high. The Paris club just lifted back-to-back European Cups, beating Arsenal in the most recent final. AC Milan’s Rafael Leão, Chelsea’s Pedro Neto and Juventus’ Francisco Conceição offer wide attacking threat. Manchester City’s Rúben Dias and Bernardo Silva anchor defence and creativity, respectively.

Group K pits Portugal against DR Congo, Uzbekistan and Colombia. The Colombia match in Miami looms as the likely decider for top spot.

27 players named plus a symbolic “plus one” tribute to the late Diogo Jota.

Forwards: João Félix (Al Nassr), Francisco Trincão (Sporting CP), Francisco Conceição (Juventus), Pedro Neto (Chelsea), Rafael Leão (AC Milan), Gonçalo Guedes (Real Sociedad), Gonçalo Ramos (PSG); Cristiano Ronaldo (Al Nassr).

From current content on today’s superstars, to casting a nostalgic eye back on the best and worst of the beautiful game from years gone by, we look to cover all angles of football news and ensure there’s enjoyable content for all types of football fans.
